title: Baldur's Gate 3
synopsis: Infected with a mind-flayer parasite, you assemble a party of misfits and explore the Forgotten Realms in the most reactive RPG ever made.

title: Bioshock
synopsis: A plane crash survivor descends into Rapture, an underwater Objectivist utopia rotting from within, with a twist that changed game storytelling.

title: Bloodborne
synopsis: A hunter stalks the nightmare streets of Yharnam in FromSoftware's most aggressive, Lovecraftian, and atmospheric work.

title: Dark Souls III
synopsis: The last Lords of Cinder refuse to link the First Flame in this punishing, cathartic capstone to the Souls trilogy.

title: Disco Elysium
synopsis: A detective with no memory investigates a murder in a failing harbour city — the most literary and politically radical RPG ever made.

title: Divinity: Original Sin 2
synopsis: Four Source Wizards compete and cooperate on a journey to godhood in a CRPG of breathtaking systemic depth.

title: Elden Ring
synopsis: FromSoftware opens up the Lands Between in an open-world masterwork co-designed with George R.R. Martin.

title: God of War
synopsis: Kratos and his son Atreus journey through the Norse realms — a furious reinvention built around one unbroken continuous shot.

title: Hades
synopsis: The prince of the Underworld fights out of Tartarus one run at a time in a roguelike that nailed the genre's storytelling problem.

title: Half-Life 2
synopsis: Gordon Freeman returns to a dystopian Earth under alien occupation in a shooter that raised the bar for environmental storytelling.

title: Hollow Knight
synopsis: A small knight explores a vast, hand-drawn underground kingdom of insects in a Metroidvania of extraordinary depth and atmosphere.

title: Mass Effect 2
synopsis: Shepard assembles a crew of specialists for a suicide mission against a galactic threat — a template for ensemble character writing in games.

title: NieR: Automata
synopsis: Android soldiers fight a proxy war against machines in a game that uses its multiple playthroughs to dismantle its own genre conventions.

title: Portal 2
synopsis: Armed with a portal gun and the increasingly unhinged AI GLaDOS, solve spatial puzzles that keep rewriting the rules.

title: Red Dead Redemption 2
synopsis: Outlaw Arthur Morgan rides through the dying American frontier, carrying the weight of a gang and a way of life that have no future.

title: Return of the Obra Dinn
synopsis: An insurance investigator boards a ghost ship and uses a magic stopwatch to determine the fate of each crew member — a puzzle masterpiece.

title: Sekiro: Shadows Die Twice
synopsis: A one-armed shinobi fights to save his kidnapped lord in FromSoftware's most demanding and rhythmically precise combat system.

title: Slay the Spire
synopsis: Climb a procedural spire with a deck of cards — the game that defined the deckbuilder roguelike genre and still hasn't been bettered.

title: The Outer Wilds
synopsis: You explore a solar system stuck in a 22-minute loop ending in supernova — a game built entirely around the joy of discovery.

title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
synopsis: Geralt of Rivia searches a war-ravaged world for his missing adopted daughter in an open-world RPG that set the bar for narrative breadth.

title: Undertale
synopsis: A child falls underground into a world of monsters and discovers that they can befriend — rather than fight — every single one.

title: Batman: Arkham City
synopsis: Half of Gotham is walled off into an open-air prison and Batman is locked inside — the best superhero game ever made.

title: Celeste
synopsis: Madeline climbs a magical mountain while confronting her inner demons in a precision platformer that earns its emotional payoff.

title: Control
synopsis: The new director of a brutalist Federal Bureau of Control fights an extradimensional invasion through one of gaming's most uncanny buildings.

title: Cyberpunk 2077
synopsis: Mercenary V navigates Night City's neon-soaked power struggles with a dead rock legend's consciousness inside their head.

title: Dead Cells
synopsis: A headless amorphous prisoner fights through a cursed island that regenerates with every death in a tight, endlessly replayable roguelite.

title: Doom Eternal
synopsis: The Doom Slayer wages war across Hell's invasion of Earth in a shooter that turns combat into a rhythm game.

title: Dragon Age: Origins
synopsis: A Grey Warden must unite fractured kingdoms against a darkspawn Blight in BioWare's last great old-school fantasy RPG.

title: Factorio
synopsis: Build and automate an ever-expanding factory on an alien planet — a strategy game whose depth is almost impossible to describe.

title: Final Fantasy VII Remake
synopsis: Cloud and AVALANCHE fight Shinra in Midgar in a lavishly reimagined retelling that expands one disc into a full game.

title: Ghost of Tsushima
synopsis: A samurai must abandon the code of the warrior to become something else entirely in order to drive the Mongol invasion from Tsushima.

title: Horizon Zero Dawn
synopsis: A hunter in a tribal future fights mechanical dinosaurs and slowly unravels the truth of what destroyed the old world.

title: Into the Breach
synopsis: Pilot mechs in bite-sized turn-based battles against alien creatures — perfect information, no randomness, only your mistakes.

title: Metal Gear Solid V: The Phantom Pain
synopsis: Big Boss builds an offshore military nation while hunting the man who destroyed everything he built — the most mechanically rich MGS.

title: Monster Hunter: World
synopsis: Track, study, and hunt increasingly colossal monsters in a world built around the deeply satisfying loop of gear improvement.

title: Ori and the Blind Forest
synopsis: A spirit guardian traverses a dying forest to restore it in one of the most visually striking Metroidvanias ever made.

title: Prey
synopsis: A researcher trapped on a space station overrun by shape-shifting aliens has to decide what kind of human they want to be.

title: Risk of Rain 2
synopsis: Survivors crash-land on an alien planet and must fight increasingly overwhelming hordes — a roguelike where power escalation is the reward.

title: Rocket League
synopsis: Rocket-powered cars play soccer in a game with an absurdly low skill floor and an almost unreachable skill ceiling.

title: Shovel Knight
synopsis: An 8-bit knight wields a shovel to battle evil in a love letter to the NES era that somehow surpasses its inspirations.

title: Sid Meier's Civilization VI
synopsis: Build an empire to stand the test of time in the deep turn-based strategy game that has ended more nights than any other.

title: Spelunky 2
synopsis: A spelunker descends into procedurally generated caves packed with traps, shops, and secrets — the most demanding roguelike ever made.

title: Stardew Valley
synopsis: You inherit a dilapidated farm and build a new life away from corporate drudgery — made entirely by one person over four years.

title: Street Fighter 6
synopsis: The best entry in the franchise in decades: a fighting game with exceptional modern controls and a surprisingly charming World Tour mode.

title: Subnautica
synopsis: You crash-land on an ocean planet and must survive by descending deeper and deeper into a gorgeous, terrifying underwater world.

title: Tekken 8
synopsis: Kazuya and Jin's generational conflict reaches its conclusion in the most aggressive and accessible Tekken to date.

title: Titanfall 2
synopsis: A militia rifleman and a stolen Titan mech forge an unlikely bond — a campaign with level design that still hasn't been topped.

title: Transistor
synopsis: A singer wields a sentient sword against the Process, a digital force reshaping her city — gorgeous, melancholy, and mechanically inventive.

title: Valheim
synopsis: A fallen Viking warrior explores the tenth Norse world, building longhouses and slaying bosses in a survival game with unusual soul.

title: XCOM 2
synopsis: Lead a guerrilla resistance against alien occupiers in a brutal turn-based strategy where every soldier you name will eventually die.
